title: Botania: Window Box
description: A little box of flowers which attaches to the side of the house; a Botania addon.
body:
Window Box is a Botania addon mainly adding new magical flowers and other content, with the following intentions:

  • Integrating Botania with newer Minecraft updates (e.g. Oxidaisel, Candysnuft)
  • Adding functionality that I found myself wanting while playing (e.g. Snapdresson, Manastar HUD)
  • Providing options for custom scenarios like skyblock or modpacks (e.g. Groundberry, portal trees)
  • Adding things which add to the feeling of the magical/botanical theme, or just for fun (e.g. Topsy Tulip, Bouquet)


Changes to existing content:

  • Manastar: Now displays a wand HUD for its targeted pool, like the Mana Pool HUD, except displaying the overall fullness of all spark-connected pools

Functional Flowers:

  • Snapdresson: Remembers a trinket loadout. When clicked, re-equips you with those trinkets, if you have them in your inventory.
        Including within a Trinket Case or Shulker Box, etc.
  • Groundberry: Turns nearby Sand into Suspicious Sand, and Gravel into Suspicious Gravel, with the associated loot.
        Intended primarily for skyblock.
  • Oxidaisel: Rapidly oxidizes nearby copper blocks.
  • Glory Incarnata: Prevents* and reverses the zombification of Piglins, Hoglins, and Villagers.
        *except for preventing villager zombification, as this happens via death.
  • Witch's Pupil: Allows remote observation of the flower when one of its Eye Berries is plucked and eaten.
  • Dispelagonium: When splashed with a potion effect, clears that effect from nearby entities for the duration of the potion.
        Also added are potions for Wither, Mining Fatigue, and Levitation.
  • Topsy Tulip: Can be placed on any side of a block, and warps gravity to let you walk on walls.
        Requires Forge GravityAPI.

Generating Flowers:

  • Candysnuft: Generates mana when nearby candles are extinguished (consuming a candle), but only in small portion sizes (blocks of at most 3 candles).
  • Queen Anelace: Sits on a square of Grass Blocks and outputs redstone signals pointing to a block in that square; generates mana if that block (and only that block) is broken.

Magical Trees:

  • Chthonic Yew: A tree with dark black wood whose hollow contains a Nether Portal; a growable alternative to manual placement.
  • Alfthorne: A great tree with vibrant green wood whose hollow contains an Alfheim Portal. Requires the same mana infusion to open.
        Intended as options for modpack progression.

Miscellaneous:

  • Bouquet: A decorative item which can bundle up to 3 flowers together.
  • Rod of the Acropolis: A survival-friendly version of the Debug Stick which can force the shape of Stairs, Fences, Walls, and Panes.
  • Rod of the Grasslands: A simple upgrade to the Rod of the Lands which creates Grass Blocks instead of Dirt.
